message:
fix(rwlock): pre-grant lock ownership during release to prevent lost wakeups and writer starvation

Restructure RwLock release and future poll logic to eliminate a class of
lost-wakeup bugs where a newly-arriving writer could steal the lock from
waiters that were already woken.

Release-side changes (release_reader, release_writer):
- release_reader: When readers drops to 0 and writers are waiting,
  eagerly set writer_active=true alongside popping the writer waker,
  so the grant is atomic with the wakeup
- release_writer: When handing off to another writer, eagerly set
  writer_active=true; when draining reader waiters, pre-increment
  state.readers by the number of wakers drained, so readers own
  their slot before being woken

ReadFuture::poll restructure:
- Check waiter_id first: if we were dequeued, our slot was already
  pre-granted (state.readers incremented for us), return Ready
- If still in queue, just update waker and return Pending
- Only try fast-path (!writer_active && writer_waiters==0) for new
  arrivals (waiter_id.is_none())

ReadFuture::drop fix:
- If our waiter_id was dequeued but we never took the lock, decrement
  state.readers (we were pre-granted but dropped) and cascade wakeup
  to next writer waiter if readers now 0

WriteFuture::poll restructure:
- Check waiter_id first: if dequeued, we were pre-granted (writer_active
  already set for us), decrement writer_waiters and return Ready
- If still in queue, update waker and return Pending
- Only try fast-path for new arrivals with writer_waiters==1

WriteFuture::drop fix:
- If waiter_id dequeued but not taken: clear writer_active, cascade
  to next writer waiter (setting writer_active) or drain readers
  (pre-incrementing state.readers)
- If waiter_id.is_none() (never registered), just decrement counter
  and cascade to readers if no more writers waiting

Lost-wakeup test updated:
- Writer 2 now correctly queues instead of stealing the lock
- Added assertion: w2_res must be None (Pending), proving pre-grant
  semantics prevent the writer-steals-from-woken-readers race

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
